mapping.a_element: symmetry-breaking chain of subalgebras of osp(5|2)
mapping.b_element: breaking of the local GUT groups SO(10)/E6 down to the Standard Model gauge group

mapping.a_element: branching of the 64-dimensional codon irrep into multiplets
mapping.b_element: decomposition of GUT representations into Standard Model matter multiplets (quark/lepton families)

brief_justification: Both invoke Lie-algebra representation theory and a symmetry-breaking chain (osp(5|2) branching in A; SO(10)/E6 GUT breaking in B), but the central driving machinery differs: A fits the genetic code's degeneracies purely by branching one large irrep along a breaking pattern, whereas B is a heterotic-string Z6-II orbifold landscape search (D-flat decoupling of exotics, string selection rules/gamma-rule, See-Saw neutrino masses) with no branching-to-reproduce-a-degeneracy logic. The shared group-theory vocabulary is a generic tool, not the same transferable mechanism.
